long_description:
Need to collect memory profiling for pod fabcon-manager-8wlhl hosted on node dal4-qz1-sr1-rk139-s40 on mzone747.

Note: Please follow the runbook and till the data collection. Pod restart is not required. Files with name starting with "profile" would be generated while running the command. Please share the files with name starting by profile.
Incident source: IBM Staff - Athul MS
short_description: Collect memory profile for fabcon-manager-8wlhl on mzone747
close_notes:
Issue Summary:
Collect memory profile for fabcon-manager-8wlhl on mzone747
SME Findings: sme-team: SME Findings
Reason of Escalation:

Signature of Issue: Collect memory profile for fabcon-manager-8wlhl on mzone747
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/genctl-network-high-cpu-memory-usage.html
Category/Subcategory: fabcon / service issue/responsivness
Timeline Summary:
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed / Not Available / URL: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/genctl-network-high-cpu-memory-usage.html
Caused by Change/Incident:
Warroom:
Meeting URL:
Outage Start:
Outage End:
current_status_next_steps:
Note: The data collection required 2 terminal sessions.

Steps to be executed:

- Login to deployer corresponding to mzone and set gt to the required mzone
- Create 2 terminals with sudo permissions (if required)
- Run on a terminal 1 :
  # kubectl port-forward -n genctl fabcon-manager-8wlhl 8888:8383

Below steps are to be executed from terminal 2. 
- On another session, terminal 2:
   # curl -d '{"type":"heap", "count": 5, "seconds": 5}' -H "X-Request-ID: 123456" -H "Content-Type:application/json" 'http://localhost:8888/diagnostics/checks/profile' > profile.json

- Parse json into .prof files:
  # cat profile.json | jq -r '.check.textValues.profilingResult | fromjson | .Profiles[]' | awk '{ filename = "profile_" NR ".prof"; cmd = "base64 -d > " filename; print $0 | cmd; close(cmd) }'

 -- runbook link -- 
https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/genctl-network-high-cpu-memory-usage.html
